observation.images.exterior_image_1_left.comment: DROID exterior (over-shoulder-left) view, GR00T modality key video.exterior_image_1_left. The env's exterior_image maps here, aspect-preserving resize+pad to 180x320 uint8 (openpi resize_with_pad), exactly like the official DROID client (examples/DROID/main_gr00t.py RESOLUTION=(180,320)).

observation.images.wrist_image_left.comment: DROID wrist view, GR00T modality key video.wrist_image_left. The env's wrist_image maps here (resize+pad to 180x320).

observation.text.comment: Task instruction. GR00T language modality key annotation.language.language_instruction (first of up to 6 duplicate slots); fed as language={key: [[instruction]]}.

observation.state.eef_9d.comment: EE pose as xyz(3) + first-two-rows-of-rotation-matrix rot6d(6), with the DROID egocentric rotation correction (examples/DROID/main_gr00t.py compute_eef_9d, DROID_EEF_ROTATION_CORRECT). OPEN: the RoboLab env does NOT expose EE cartesian pose in its wire state (only 7 joints + gripper), so the agent ZERO-FILLS eef_9d. GR00T's relative-joint action is reconstructed from joint_position state (which IS available), so the emitted joint action stays valid; eef_9d only conditions the VLM and is mitigated by the checkpoint's 0.5 state-dropout. For full fidelity, extend the RoboLab bridge to emit cartesian_position and compute eef_9d here.

observation.state.gripper_position.comment: Gripper opening (GR00T state key gripper_position, 0=open..1=closed). The RoboLab env's gripper_pos (NORM01, 0=open/1=closed) maps directly.

observation.state.joint_position.comment: 7 absolute Panda arm joint positions (rad). The RoboLab env's 7 arm joints map directly; this is the state GR00T uses to convert its relative-joint action back to absolute targets at inference time.

action.joint_position.comment: 7 Panda arm joint position targets. The embodiment is `..._relative_eef_relative_joint`, but get_action returns ABSOLUTE joint targets (q01/q99 here span the Franka joint range — GR00T applies the inverse relative transform using the joint_position STATE the agent feeds). The official DROID client (main_gr00t.py) sends them straight to env.step (action_space='joint_position'); the agent does the same to RoboLab's DroidJointPositionActionCfg. NB: GR00T also emits eef_9d (9) action dims, which we DISCARD — the env consumes joint_position+gripper only (this contract specs the real sent action).

action.gripper.comment: Absolute gripper command (GR00T action key gripper_position). Binarized >0.5 -> close, exactly like the official client; RoboLab's BinaryJointPositionZeroToOneActionCfg closes the finger_joint when >0.5.

comment: NVIDIA Isaac GR00T N1.7 post-trained on DROID (nvidia/GR00T-N1.7-DROID, EmbodimentTag.OXE_DROID_RELATIVE_EEF_RELATIVE_JOINT). Cosmos-Reason2-2B VLM backbone + flow-matching DiT (4 denoising steps, 3B params). Inference via Gr00tPolicy.get_action({'video':{exterior_image_1_left,wrist_image_left},'state':{eef_9d,gripper_position,joint_position},'language':{...}}) -> action chunk (T=40) of {eef_9d(9),gripper_position(1),joint_position(7)}; the agent uses joint_position(7)+gripper(1) and discards eef_9d. Action signature JOINT_ABS_POS+GRIPPER_ABS_POS matches the RoboLab env joint_pos branch (robolab.json). Open-loop horizon 15 (official DROID client), ~15 Hz. Pairs the `robolab` env launched with --control joint_pos (the default). KEY OPEN: the env does not expose EE cartesian pose, so the eef_9d state input is zero-filled (see observation.state.eef_9d). Requires the `gr00t` package (NVIDIA/Isaac-GR00T); not a LeRobot policy (custom GR00TModel).
